source_article: "BBC Monitoring,1995-07-24,_F Source: Radio Burundi, Bujumbura, in French 1800 gmt 22 Jul 95   Excerpts from report by Burundi radio on 22nd July   The Kiriri University campus [in Bujumbura] was attacked by armed groups last night. The death toll from the attack is eight people killed. The dead include four students and two workers on the campus. Two of the dead were assailants shot down by the security forces. Three students were wounded...   Still on insecurity, six members of one family were last night killed in house No 9 on the Sixth Avenue in Bwiza [commune near the city of Bujumbura].   (c) BBC Monitoring Summary of World Broadcasts."

source_article: "E/CN.4/1997/12/Add.1,1997-03-07,Second report on the human rights situation in Burundi submitted by the Special Rapporteur, Mr. Paulo Sérgio Pinheiro, in accordance with Commission resolution 1996/1";"BBC Monitoring,1996-07-05,Body "Source: Radio Burundi, Bujumbura, in French 1800 gmt 3 Jul 96   Text of report by Burundi radio on 3rd July   Security has been jeopardised this morning [3rd July] at the Teza tea centre situated in Muramvya Province [centre]. The damage is enormous.   According to military sources the factory [word indistinct] and vehicles were damaged. There have also been losses in human lives. Some employees of the Teza tea factory were killed and others injured. Two soldiers were injured and many assailants killed. Eight Kalashnikov rifles belonging to the assailants were recovered by security forces. The assailants later withdrew towards Musigati.   Also, according to military sources, an ambush occurred today on National Road No 1 on a bus transporting civilians. The bus was heading to the interior of the country. Two people were killed and two others injured.   (c) BBC Monitoring Summary of World Broadcasts.""
source_headline: Second report on the human rights situation in Burundi submitted by the Special Rapporteur, Mr. Paulo Sérgio Pinheiro, in accordance with Commission resolution 1996/1;Body "Source: Radio Burundi, Bujumbura, in French 1800 gmt 3 Jul 96   Text of report by Burundi radio on 3rd July   Security has been jeopardised this morning [3rd July] at the Teza tea centre situated in Muramvya Province [centre]. The damage is enormous.   According to military sources the factory [word indistinct] and vehicles were damaged. There have also been losses in human lives. Some employees of the Teza tea factory were killed and others injured. Two soldiers were injured and many assailants killed. Eight Kalashnikov rifles belonging to the assailants were recovered by security forces. The assailants later withdrew towards Musigati.   Also, according to military sources, an ambush occurred today on National Road No 1 on a bus transporting civilians. The bus was heading to the interior of the country. Two people were killed and two others injured.   (c) BBC Monitoring Summary of World Broadcasts."

source_article: "BBC Monitoring Summary of World Broadcasts,1999-07-28,Body "Source: Azania news agency, Bujumbura, in French 26 Jul 99   Text of report by Burundian news agency Azania on 26th July   [No dateline, as received] The rebels are withdrawing in search of new hide-outs. Two soldiers were killed and one wounded in Murinda when a vehicle was ambushed.   In Gatabo, where a concentration of rebels was reported, one soldier and three civilians were wounded during pursuit operations. Three civilians were taken hostage by rebels on Saturday night [24th July]. In the commune of Nyanda four presumed rebels were taken in for questioning, a local authority said. They are suspected of complicity with the enemy.   On Saturday 24th July a rebel attack on Karonde market in the Kabezi zone in Bujumbura-Rural Province led to the killing of three civilians, according to local eye-witnesses.   (c) BBC Monitoring Summary of World Broadcasts."";"NET PRESS,1999-07-24,Burundi 24 julliet 1999"
